title: Combat and Spawning — Game Mechanics
description: Create a Monster class with name, health, max_health, attack, defense, loot_table (list of item name strings), and respawn_time (seconds). Create a SpawnManager that tracks monster positions in rooms and respawns them after their timer expires. Create a CombatEngine with attack(attacker, defender) that calculates damage as max(1, attacker.attack - defender.defense), apply_damage(target, amount), and drop_loot(monster) that returns a random item from loot_table. Use os.getpid() and sys.platform in a log_combat() helper.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 9: Combat and Spawning
import os
import sys
import random

assert 'Monster' in dir(), 'Monster class must be defined'
assert 'SpawnManager' in dir(), 'SpawnManager class must be defined'
assert 'CombatEngine' in dir(), 'CombatEngine class must be defined'

# Test Monster constructor
monster = Monster('Goblin', 50, 10, 5, ['gold coin', 'rusty dagger'], 30)
assert monster.name == 'Goblin', 'Monster should store name'
assert monster.health == 50, 'Monster should store health'
assert monster.max_health == 50, 'max_health should equal initial health'
assert monster.attack == 10, 'Monster should store attack'
assert monster.defense == 5, 'Monster should store defense'
assert monster.loot_table == ['gold coin', 'rusty dagger'], 'Monster should store loot_table'
assert monster.respawn_time == 30, 'Monster should store respawn_time'

# Test Monster defaults
monster2 = Monster('Rat', 20, 3, 1)
assert monster2.loot_table == [], 'Default loot_table should be empty'
assert monster2.respawn_time == 60, 'Default respawn_time should be 60'

# Test is_alive
assert monster.is_alive() is True, 'Monster with health > 0 should be alive'

# Test take_damage
monster.take_damage(20)
assert monster.health == 30, f'Health should be 30 after 20 damage, got {monster.health}'
monster.take_damage(100)
assert monster.health == 0, 'Health should not go below 0'
assert monster.is_alive() is False, 'Monster with 0 health should not be alive'

# Test respawn
monster.respawn()
assert monster.health == 50, f'Health should be 50 after respawn, got {monster.health}'
assert monster.is_alive() is True, 'Monster should be alive after respawn'

# Test SpawnManager
mgr = SpawnManager()
assert mgr.total_spawns == 0, 'Should start with 0 spawns'

goblin = Monster('Goblin', 50, 10, 5, ['gold coin'])
rat = Monster('Rat', 20, 3, 1)

mgr.spawn_monster(goblin, 'dark_cave')
mgr.spawn_monster(rat, 'dark_cave')
assert mgr.total_spawns == 2, f'Should have 2 spawns, got {mgr.total_spawns}'

# Test get_monsters
monsters = mgr.get_monsters('dark_cave')
assert len(monsters) == 2, f'Should have 2 monsters in dark_cave, got {len(monsters)}'
assert goblin in monsters, 'Goblin should be in dark_cave'

# Test get_monsters for empty room
assert mgr.get_monsters('empty_room') == [], 'Empty room should have no monsters'

# Test mark_dead
mgr.mark_dead(goblin, 'dark_cave')
assert mgr.total_spawns == 1, f'Should have 1 spawn after killing goblin, got {mgr.total_spawns}'

pending = mgr.get_pending_respawns()
assert len(pending) == 1, f'Should have 1 pending respawn, got {len(pending)}'
assert pending[0][0] is goblin, 'Pending respawn should be the goblin'
assert pending[0][1] == 'dark_cave', 'Pending respawn room should be dark_cave'

# Test CombatEngine
engine = CombatEngine()

warrior = Character('Hero', 'warrior', 120, 15, 12)
target = Monster('Slime', 30, 5, 3)

# Test attack
damage = engine.attack(warrior, target)
assert damage == 12, f'Damage should be 15-3=12, got {damage}'  # warrior.attack(15) - slime.defense(3)
assert target.health == 18, f'Slime health should be 30-12=18, got {target.health}'

# Test minimum damage of 1
tank = Monster('Tank', 100, 5, 50)
damage2 = engine.attack(warrior, tank)
assert damage2 == 1, f'Minimum damage should be 1, got {damage2}'  # 15-50 = -35, clamped to 1

# Test drop_loot
loot_monster = Monster('Dragon', 500, 50, 30, ['dragon scale', 'gold pile', 'magic gem'])
random.seed(42)
loot = engine.drop_loot(loot_monster)
assert loot in loot_monster.loot_table, f'Loot should be from loot_table, got {loot}'

# Test drop_loot with empty table
empty_monster = Monster('Ghost', 10, 1, 0)
assert engine.drop_loot(empty_monster) is None, 'Empty loot_table should return None'

# Test log_combat
log = engine.log_combat('Hero', 'Goblin', 12)
assert str(os.getpid()) in log, 'Log should contain PID'
assert sys.platform in log, 'Log should contain platform'
assert 'Hero' in log, 'Log should contain attacker name'
assert 'Goblin' in log, 'Log should contain defender name'
assert '12' in log, 'Log should contain damage amount'
acceptance_criteria: Monster stores name, health, max_health, attack, defense, loot_table, respawn_time
Monster.is_alive(), take_damage(), and respawn() work correctly
SpawnManager.get_monsters() returns living monsters in a room
SpawnManager.mark_dead() removes monster and tracks for respawn
CombatEngine.attack() calculates damage as max(1, attack - defense)
CombatEngine.drop_loot() returns random item or None for empty table
hints: Monster is similar to Character: store attributes, clamp health with max(0, ...).
In SpawnManager, use a defaultdict(list) or check 'if room_name not in self._spawns'.
In mark_dead, use self._spawns[room_name].remove(monster) to remove from room.
CombatEngine.attack: damage = max(1, attacker.attack - defender.defense) ensures at least 1 damage.
